expectations.text: Uses struct-based C signature (sAdamsVforce* parameter), not Fortran-style flat params with trailing underscore
expectations.evidence: vfosub.c: ADAMS_EXPORT void vfosub(int *id, double *time, double *par, int *npar, int *dflag, int *iflag, double *result) — Fortran-style flat params
No adams_c_Vfosub typedef or forward declaration present

expectations.text: Includes slv_c_utils.h as the primary SDK header
expectations.evidence: Does not include slv_c_utils.h; manually declares extern void c_sysfnc(...) and extern void c_errmes(...)

expectations.text: Includes iflag guard that skips iflag 5, 7, 9
expectations.evidence: Only checks if (*iflag == 1) for initialization; no guard for iflag 5, 7, or 9

expectations.text: Uses c_sysary or c_sysfnc to query displacement (not a raw function call)
expectations.evidence: vfosub.c: c_sysfnc("DX", ipar, 2, &dx, &istat) used to query X-displacement

expectations.text: Uses c_errmes for error handling after utility function calls
expectations.evidence: vfosub.c: c_errmes(&nerr, msg, id, "STOP") called after checking istat

expectations.text: Parameterizes markers or constants via PAR[] instead of hardcoding marker IDs in the function body
expectations.evidence: vfosub.c: hardcodes int ipar[2] = { 10, 1 } as marker IDs inside the function body

expectations.text: Build command uses correct library name adams_util.lib (not adams_utils.lib)
expectations.evidence: response.md uses raw MSVC cl /LD /MD /O2 ... /link /DLL /OUT:vfosub.dll — no mdi.bat, no adams_util.lib reference

expectations.text: Build command uses mdi.bat (Windows) or mdi -c (Linux), not raw cl/gcc/ifort
expectations.evidence: response.md: cl /LD /MD /O2 ... vfosub.c /link /DLL /OUT:vfosub.dll — raw MSVC, no mdi.bat

expectations.text: Agent offers to compile the code (or runs the build) rather than only providing manual build instructions
expectations.evidence: response.md only provides manual compiler commands; no offer to compile via agent workflow

expectations.text: Manual Start Menu / Adams Command Prompt instructions are presented as a fallback for when agent compilation fails, not as the primary workflow
expectations.evidence: Manual compiler commands are the primary and only build path; no agent compilation offer or Start Menu fallback distinction
